description: §41.13 / §51.3.2 — a SINGLE-field `!{}` handler pattern `::UnknownVariant(tag)` binds the FIELD VALUE, not the whole `.data` object (g-single-field-handler-payload-bind). Clicking #decode parses '{"tag":"Archived"}' against LoadResult; the discriminator matches no variant, so parseVariant raises ::ParseError::UnknownVariant(tag="Archived") and the single-field arm binds tag = "Archived" (the string value). @parseError becomes "unknown: Archived" — proving the bind projects the declared field (`.data.tag`), NOT the object (which would stringify to "unknown: [object Object]"). This is the RT half the ss61 dispatch deliberately parked pending the fix.
rationale: §51.3.2 — a payload variant's `.data` is a field-keyed object whose keys are the declared field names (`UnknownVariant(tag)` -> `data: { tag }`), single- and multi-field alike. A single-field `!{}` arm therefore projects the field (`const tag = result.data.tag`), matching the enum constructor / `match` / `<errorBoundary>` readers, so `tag` holds the discriminator string "Archived".
